name: Retime the PTX barrier seam on the current correctness-safe 128x128 anchor

summary.name: Retime the PTX barrier seam on the current correctness-safe 128x128 anchor
summary.hypothesis: The last two writer-family iterations both landed back on the same 198-register / 16.6%-active-warps regime, so the cheapest remaining local seam on the correct anchor is the cp.async wait-group plus CTA barrier handoff. A bounded retime can target the persistent 5.95% barrier stall and 6.85% long-scoreboard stall without touching writer coverage or geometry.
summary.expected_bottleneck: synchronization_barrier_issue layered on top of occupancy_latency_hiding_issue in the current correctness-safe 128x128 PTX anchor

score_breakdown.reason: Best current-anchor exploit after the writer-shape family flattened out.

ranking_notes: This is now the cleanest local seam on the current correctness-safe anchor.
It does not depend on preserving the round-7 low-register writer signal.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if barrier retiming breaks correctness, grows shared memory, or improves barrier without any runtime win.

target_hotspots.explanation: Barrier remains the most actionable local stall seam on the current correct anchor.
Any handoff retime must not simply trade barrier time for worse scoreboard pressure.

expected_local_changes: Retune cp.async wait-group completion versus CTA-wide sync on the steady-state PTX path.
Keep writer coverage and the 22016-byte shared-memory budget fixed.
Do not mix barrier work with another writer sweep rewrite.

guardrail_metrics.reason: This stays on the current correctness-safe anchor.
Do not improve barrier by worsening scoreboard stalls.

notes: Node C may now implement this one candidate.
